message:
feat(server): concurrent read-only HTTP request handling via session snapshots

Introduce HttpRequestExecutionMode to classify JSON-RPC methods as either
ConcurrentReadOnly (tools/call, resources/read, prompts/get) or
ExclusiveSession (everything else). Read-only methods take a lightweight
SessionSnapshot from the session mutex and release it immediately, so
handler execution for these high-frequency methods is not serialized on
the session lock.

SessionSnapshot captures initialized state, SessionState, sampling/
elicitation capability flags, and log level. A new
handle_request_with_snapshot() method dispatches read-only requests using
the snapshot instead of holding the mutex for the full handler duration.

Exclusive methods (initialize, notifications, completion, logging) still
hold the full session lock for mutation safety.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
